messages.content: Always use code blocks with the appropriate language tags. If asked for a table always format it using Markdown syntax.
Hello! How can I assist you today?

It seems like you want to know the maximum value that can be stored in an int8 data type. In MATLAB, an int8 data type is an 8-bit signed integer type that can hold values in the range from -128 to 127.

You can access the maximum value programmatically in MATLAB by using the `intmax` function specifically for int8 data type. 
Here is an example code snippet to get the maximum value for int8 data type in MATLAB:

```matlab
max_int8 = intmax('int8');
disp(max_int8);
```

When you run this code snippet in MATLAB, it will display the maximum value that can be stored in an int8 data type, which is 127.
